title: Agency Workers Regulations 2010
warmSubtitle: The Agency Workers Regulations 2010 give agency workers the entitlement to the same basic employment and working conditions as if they had been recruited directly by the hirer, after completing a 12-w…
chapters: SI 2010/93 · Made 20 January 2010 · In Force 1 October 2011 · Implements Temporary Agency Work Directive 2008/104/EC · Amended by Agency Workers (Amendment) Regulations 2019 (SI 2019/724) — Swedish derogation repealed 6 April 2020

title: Carer's Leave Act 2023
warmSubtitle: 3 sections + Schedule (inserting new ss.80J–80N into the Employment Rights Act 1996), supplemented by the Carer's Leave Regulations 2024 (SI 2024/468).
chapters: 3 sections + Schedule (inserting new ss.80J–80N into the Employment Rights Act 1996), supplemented by the Carer's Leave Regulations 2024 (SI 2024/468)

title: Children and Families Act 2014
jurisdiction: England and Wales (with some UK-wide employment provisions)
warmSubtitle: 139 sections + 7 Schedules in 10 Parts.
chapters: 139 sections + 7 Schedules in 10 Parts. A broad omnibus Act covering children's social care, SEN, adoption, family justice, and employment. The EMPLOYMENT-RELEVANT provisions are concentrated in Parts 7-9: Part 7 (ss.117-123) — SHARED PARENTAL LEAVE AND PAY, Part 8 (ss.124-131) — TIME OFF FOR ANTENATAL APPOINTMENTS AND ADOPTION MEETINGS, Part 9 (ss.131A-132) — RIGHT TO REQUEST FLEXIBLE WORKING EXTENDED TO ALL EMPLOYEES. These provisions insert new sections into the ERA 1996 and amend the Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992. Implemented by the Shared Parental Leave Regulations 2014 (SI 2014/3050) and the Statutory Shared Parental Pay (General) Regulations 2014 (SI 2014/3051).

title: Collective Redundancies and Transfer of Undertakings (Protection of Employment) (Amendment) Regulations 2014
jurisdiction: England, Wales, and Scotland (not Northern Ireland)
warmSubtitle: 11 regulations.
chapters: 11 regulations. Amends BOTH TULRCA 1992 (collective redundancy consultation) AND TUPE 2006 (transfer of undertakings). Six key amendments: (1) pre-transfer collective redundancy consultation by the transferee, (2) service provision change clarification, (3) greater flexibility to vary terms post-transfer, (4) static approach to collective agreements, (5) employee liability information deadline extended to 28 days, (6) micro-business direct consultation. CRITICAL INTERPLAY NOTE: This instrument sits at the intersection of THREE major statutory frameworks — collective redundancy (TULRCA s.188), TUPE (SI 2006/246), and unfair dismissal (ERA 1996 s.98). The ERA 2025 introduces further changes to collective redundancy consultation (2027) that will affect how this instrument operates.

title: Construction (Design and Management) Regulations 2015
chapters: SI 2015/51. 39 Regulations + 5 Schedules in 5 Parts. Made under HSWA 1974 ss.15, 80. Replaced CDM 2007 from 6 April 2015. Supported by HSE guidance L153 'Managing health and safety in construction'. CDM 2015 applies to ALL construction work — there is no minimum project value or duration threshold. Even small works such as office refurbishments, shop fit-outs, and building maintenance are within scope. The Regulations define FIVE duty holders: client, principal designer, principal contractor, designers, and contractors. An employer who commissions construction work is a CLIENT and has legal duties, regardless of whether construction is the employer's core business.

title: Control of Substances Hazardous to Health Regulations 2002
chapters: SI 2002/2677. 21 Regulations + 6 Schedules. Made under HSWA 1974 ss.15(1), 15(2), 15(3)(a), 15(3)(c), 15(4), 15(5)(a), 80(1), 82(3)(a). Implements multiple EU Directives on chemical agents, carcinogens, and biological agents. Supported by HSE ACOP L5 (6th edition). The Regulations impose a comprehensive duty on employers to ASSESS, PREVENT or CONTROL, MONITOR, SURVEY, and TRAIN in relation to any substance hazardous to health encountered in the workplace. Breach is a criminal offence — unlimited fine on indictment.

title: Data Protection Act 2018
warmSubtitle: 215 sections + 19 Schedules across 7 Parts
chapters: 215 sections + 19 Schedules across 7 Parts

title: Deddf Llesiant Cenedlaethau'r Dyfodol (Cymru) 2015 / Well-being of Future Generations (Wales) Act 2015
short: Deddf Llesiant Cenedlaethau'r Dyfodol / Well-being of Future Generations Act 2015
type: Primary Legislation — Senedd Cymru / Welsh Parliament (formerly National Assembly for Wales)
warmSubtitle: 53 adran / sections mewn 5 Rhan / in 5 Parts + 4 Atodlen / Schedules.
chapters: 53 adran / sections mewn 5 Rhan / in 5 Parts + 4 Atodlen / Schedules. Internationally recognised as one of the most holistic pieces of sustainable development legislation worldwide. Establishes 7 well-being goals, a sustainable development principle (5 ways of working), a well-being duty on 44+ public bodies, a Future Generations Commissioner, and Public Services Boards. AMENDED by the Social Partnership and Public Procurement (Wales) Act 2023 — which substituted 'FAIR WORK' (gwaith teg) for 'decent work' in the 'Prosperous Wales' goal. This amendment is the STATUTORY BRIDGE between the Well-being framework and employment law in Wales. The SPPP Act's Social Partnership Duty and socially responsible procurement duties are exercised THROUGH the well-being objectives framework established by THIS Act. Understanding this Act is prerequisite to understanding every other Welsh instrument in the KL estate.

title: Deddf Partneriaeth Gymdeithasol a Chaffael Cyhoeddus (Cymru) 2023 / Social Partnership and Public Procurement (Wales) Act 2023
short: Deddf Partneriaeth Gymdeithasol / Social Partnership Act 2023
type: Primary Legislation — Senedd Cymru / Welsh Parliament
warmSubtitle: 49 adran / sections mewn 4 Rhan / in 4 Parts + 2 Atodlen / Schedules.
chapters: 49 adran / sections mewn 4 Rhan / in 4 Parts + 2 Atodlen / Schedules. Deddfwriaeth arloesol / Pioneering legislation — the first UK Act to embed social partnership, fair work, and socially responsible procurement in statute. TEMPORAL STATUS: Commenced in stages through 4 Commencement Orders. The FINAL remaining provisions commenced 25 MARCH 2026 (Commencement No. 4 Order 2025). Reporting duties commence 1 APRIL 2026. The Act is NOW SUBSTANTIALLY IN FORCE. This is the most temporally immediate instrument in the entire KL estate.

title: Deddf y Sector Amaethyddol (Cymru) 2014 / Agricultural Sector (Wales) Act 2014
short: Deddf y Sector Amaethyddol / Agricultural Sector Act 2014
type: Primary Legislation — Senedd Cymru / Welsh Parliament (enacted as Emergency Bill)
warmSubtitle: 19 adran / sections.
chapters: 19 adran / sections. An emergency Act passed in response to the UK Government's abolition of the Agricultural Wages Board for England and Wales (ERRA 2013 s.72). The Welsh Assembly (now Senedd Cymru) legislated to PRESERVE agricultural worker protections in Wales. The UK Attorney General challenged the Assembly's competence — the Supreme Court ruled in Wales's favour. This Act is therefore both an EMPLOYMENT PROTECTION instrument and a CONSTITUTIONAL LANDMARK in Welsh devolution. It establishes the Agricultural Advisory Panel for Wales, provides for agricultural wages orders setting minimum rates ABOVE the NMW/NLW, and promotes careers and skills in the agricultural sector.

title: Employment (Allocation of Tips) Act 2023
warmSubtitle: 15 sections (inserting new ss.27D–27Q into the Employment Rights Act 1996).
chapters: 15 sections (inserting new ss.27D–27Q into the Employment Rights Act 1996)

title: Employment Act 2002
warmSubtitle: 53 sections + 9 Schedules in 4 Parts.
chapters: 53 sections + 9 Schedules in 4 Parts. A major omnibus Act covering paternity/adoption leave and pay, maternity reforms, statutory dispute resolution, flexible working, written statement requirements, fixed-term employees, and union learning representatives. CRITICAL TEMPORAL NOTE: This Act is PARTIALLY SUPERSEDED. Part 3 (statutory dispute resolution — ss.29-33 + Schedules 2-4) was REPEALED by the Employment Act 2008 after the Gibbons Review concluded it was a legislative FAILURE that actually INCREASED rather than reduced tribunal claims. Parts 1-2 (family leave) remain in force but have been HEAVILY AMENDED. s.38 (written statement award) remains live and is being amended by ERA 2025.

title: Employment Act 2008
warmSubtitle: 20 sections.
chapters: 20 sections. A focused CORRECTIVE Act with three pillars: (1) DISPUTE RESOLUTION — repeal of the failed EA 2002 statutory procedures and establishment of the ACAS Code 25% adjustment mechanism (ss.1-7); (2) NATIONAL MINIMUM WAGE ENFORCEMENT — strengthened enforcement framework (ss.8-12); (3) EMPLOYMENT AGENCY ENFORCEMENT — enhanced powers for the Employment Agency Standards Inspectorate (ss.13-18). Plus s.19: trade union membership amendment following ASLEF v UK. CRITICAL EVOLUTIONARY NOTE: This Act sits at the PIVOT POINT of UK dispute resolution law. It ended the failed experiment of statutory procedures (EA 2002) and RESTORED the principles-based approach (ACAS Code + Polkey) that the ERA 2025 now builds upon. Its NMW and agency enforcement provisions are being CONSOLIDATED into the Fair Work Agency (April 2026).

title: Employment Rights Act 1996
warmSubtitle: The principal statute governing individual employment rights in Great Britain.
chapters: 246 sections + Schedules

title: Employment Rights Act 1996 (Section 1 Statement) — as amended by the Employment Rights (Employment Particulars and Paid Annual Leave) (Amendment) Regulations 2018 and the Employment Rights (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 2019
warmSubtitle: Two implementing instruments: (1) SI 2018/1378 — Employment Rights (Employment Particulars and Paid Annual Leave) (Amendment) Regulations 2018, and (2) SI 2019/731 — Employment Rights (Miscellaneous A…
chapters: Two implementing instruments: (1) SI 2018/1378 — Employment Rights (Employment Particulars and Paid Annual Leave) (Amendment) Regulations 2018, and (2) SI 2019/731 — Employment Rights (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 2019. Together they transform the ERA 1996 s.1 written statement of employment particulars from a document that could be provided within 2 months of starting work (to employees only) into a DAY-ONE RIGHT that must be provided on or before the first day of work to BOTH employees AND workers, with significantly expanded content requirements.

title: Employment Rights Act 2025
warmSubtitle: The most significant reform of UK employment rights in a generation.
chapters: 151 sections + Schedules

title: Employment Tribunals Act 1996
warmSubtitle: 48 sections in 3 Parts (Part I — Employment Tribunals, Part II — Employment Appeal Tribunal, Part III — Supplementary), supplemented by the Employment Tribunal Procedure Rules 2024 (SI 2024/1155, in f…
chapters: 48 sections in 3 Parts (Part I — Employment Tribunals, Part II — Employment Appeal Tribunal, Part III — Supplementary), supplemented by the Employment Tribunal Procedure Rules 2024 (SI 2024/1155, in force 6 January 2025)

title: Enterprise and Regulatory Reform Act 2013
warmSubtitle: 103 sections + 22 Schedules.
chapters: 103 sections + 22 Schedules. A broad Coalition Government Act covering competition, regulation, and employment. The EMPLOYMENT provisions (Part 2, ss.7-25) are among the most practically impactful in everyday HR management. Five key pillars: (1) PROTECTED CONVERSATIONS — s.111A ERA 1996 enabling confidential exit discussions; (2) MANDATORY ACAS EARLY CONCILIATION — no tribunal claim without first contacting ACAS; (3) FINANCIAL PENALTIES for employers with aggravating features; (4) WHISTLEBLOWING REFORM — public interest test; (5) H&S CIVIL LIABILITY — removal of employee right to sue for breach of H&S statutory duty. The ERA 2025 builds on pillars 1-4 while the controversial pillar 5 remains unchanged.

title: Fixed-Term Employees (Prevention of Less Favourable Treatment) Regulations 2002
warmSubtitle: The Fixed-Term Employees (Prevention of Less Favourable Treatment) Regulations 2002 protect fixed-term employees from less favourable treatment compared to comparable permanent employees, unless the t…
chapters: SI 2002/2034 · Made 24 July 2002 · In Force 1 October 2002 · Implements Fixed-Term Work Directive 1999/70/EC

title: Flexible Working Regulations 2014
warmSubtitle: The Flexible Working Regulations 2014 replaced the statutory procedure for flexible working requests with a duty on the employer to deal with requests in a reasonable manner.
chapters: SI 2014/1398 · In Force 30 June 2014 · Made under ERA 1996 s.80F–80I · Amended by Employment Relations (Flexible Working) Act 2023 (from 6 April 2024) · Supplemented by ACAS Code of Practice 5 (from 6 April 2024)

title: Health and Safety at Work etc. Act 1974
warmSubtitle: The Health and Safety at Work etc.
chapters: Chapter 37 (1974) · Royal Assent 31 July 1974 · In Force 1 April 1975 · 85 Sections + 10 Schedules · Primary enabling Act for all UK health and safety regulation · Based on the Robens Committee Report 1972

title: Human Rights Act 1998
warmSubtitle: 22 sections + 4 Schedules.
chapters: 22 sections + 4 Schedules. Schedule 1 sets out the Convention Rights. The Act incorporates most rights in the European Convention on Human Rights (ECHR) into domestic UK law. Widely regarded as a constitutional statute — it cannot be impliedly repealed (Thoburn v Sunderland City Council [2002]).

title: Immigration Act 2014
jurisdiction: United Kingdom (right to rent provisions: England only as at March 2026)
warmSubtitle: 77 sections + 9 Schedules in 7 Parts.
chapters: 77 sections + 9 Schedules in 7 Parts. Part 1 (ss.1-14): Removal. Part 2 (ss.15-19): Appeals. Part 3 (ss.20-48): Access to Services — the employer-critical Part covering right to rent, employment enforcement, NHS surcharge, bank accounts, and driving licences. Part 4 (ss.49-56): Marriage and civil partnerships. Part 5 (ss.57-68): Oversight, enforcement, and nationality. Part 6 (s.69): Embarkation checks. Part 7 (ss.70-77): Miscellaneous including the Immigration Skills Charge power (s.70A, inserted by IA 2016).

title: Immigration, Asylum and Nationality Act 2006
warmSubtitle: 62 sections in 3 Parts.
chapters: 62 sections in 3 Parts. Part 1 (ss.1-14): Appeals and removal. Part 2 (ss.15-26): Employment — the employer obligations framework. Part 3 (ss.27-62): Information, claimants andடுother matters. Employment provisions (ss.15-25) are the core employer-facing provisions, supplemented by the Immigration (Restrictions on Employment) Order 2007 (SI 2007/3290), the Code of Practice on Preventing Illegal Working (13 February 2024), and the Home Office Employer Right to Work Guidance (updated 26 June 2025).

title: Information and Consultation of Employees Regulations 2004
chapters: SI 2004/3426. 40 Regulations in 9 Parts. Implements EU Directive 2002/14/EC (Information and Consultation Directive). Amended by Employment Rights (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 2019 (SI 2019/731) — reduced employee request threshold from 10% to 2%. The Regulations remain in force as retained EU law post-Brexit.

title: Insolvency Act 1986
jurisdiction: England and Wales (with parallel provisions for Scotland)
warmSubtitle: 444 sections + 14 Schedules in 19 Parts.
chapters: 444 sections + 14 Schedules in 19 Parts. Employment-relevant provisions centred on: Part XII (ss.386-387 + Schedule 6 — preferential debts including employee claims), Part I (ss.1-7 — Company Voluntary Arrangements), Part II (ss.8-27 + Schedule B1 — Administration), Part IV (ss.73-160 — Winding Up/Liquidation). Employee claims for unpaid debts are governed by ERA 1996 Part XII (ss.182-190) which operates alongside the IA 1986 creditor priority framework. The Regulations must be read together with TUPE 2006 (protection of employees on business transfer from an insolvent employer) and the Pensions Act 2004 (Pension Protection Fund).

title: Management of Health and Safety at Work Regulations 1999
warmSubtitle: The Management of Health and Safety at Work Regulations 1999 are the principal subordinate regulations under HSWA 1974, implementing EU Framework Directive 89/391/EEC.
chapters: SI 1999/3242 · In Force 29 December 1999 · Replaced MHSWR 1992 (SI 1992/2051) · Implements EU Framework Directive 89/391/EEC · Made under HSWA 1974

title: Maternity and Parental Leave etc. Regulations 1999
warmSubtitle: The Maternity and Parental Leave etc.
chapters: SI 1999/3312 · Made 10 December 1999 · In Force 15 December 1999 (Parts I–III) and 15 January 2000 (Part IV) · Made under ERA 1996 ss.71–80 and s.236 · Amended by the Maternity and Parental Leave etc. and the Paternity and Adoption Leave (Amendment) Regulations 2008 (SI 2008/1966), the Maternity and Parental Leave (Amendment) Regulations 2014 (SI 2014/3221), the Shared Parental Leave Regulations 2014 (SI 2014/3050), the Parental Leave (EU Directive) Regulations 2013 (SI 2013/283), and the Employment Rights Act 2025

title: Mesur y Gymraeg (Cymru) 2011 a Safonau'r Gymraeg / Welsh Language (Wales) Measure 2011 and Welsh Language Standards
short: Mesur y Gymraeg a'r Safonau / Welsh Language Measure and Standards
warmSubtitle: This file covers TWO interlinked instruments as an integrated system: (1) Mesur y Gymraeg (Cymru) 2011 / Welsh Language (Wales) Measure 2011 (mwa/2011/1) — the primary legislation establishing the Wel…
chapters: This file covers TWO interlinked instruments as an integrated system: (1) Mesur y Gymraeg (Cymru) 2011 / Welsh Language (Wales) Measure 2011 (mwa/2011/1) — the primary legislation establishing the Welsh language's official status, creating the Welsh Language Commissioner (Comisiynydd y Gymraeg), and providing the framework for Welsh language standards. 156 sections in 10 Parts + 13 Schedules. (2) Rheoliadau Safonau'r Gymraeg / Welsh Language Standards Regulations — a series of statutory instruments (No. 1 (2015, wsi/2015/996), No. 2 (2016), No. 4 (2016), No. 5 (2017), No. 6 (2017), No. 7 (2018), amended 2025) specifying 176 individual standards across 5 categories. The Measure creates the FRAMEWORK. The Standards provide the OPERATIONAL DETAIL. Together they form the most comprehensive statutory Welsh language protection regime in history.

title: National Minimum Wage Act 1998
warmSubtitle: The National Minimum Wage Act 1998 establishes the statutory framework for a minimum hourly wage rate across the United Kingdom.
chapters: Chapter 39 (1998) · Royal Assent 31 July 1998 · In Force 1 April 1999 · 54 Sections + Schedules

title: National Minimum Wage Regulations 2015
warmSubtitle: The National Minimum Wage Regulations 2015 provide the detailed framework for calculating whether the national minimum wage has been paid.
chapters: SI 2015/621 · Made 12 March 2015 · In Force 6 April 2015 · Replaced NMW Regulations 1999 (SI 1999/584) · Made under NMWA 1998 ss.1–4, 51–54 · Rates updated annually by National Minimum Wage (Amendment) Regulations

title: Neonatal Care (Leave and Pay) Act 2023
warmSubtitle: 6 sections (enabling Act), supplemented by the Neonatal Care Leave and Miscellaneous Amendments Regulations 2025 (SI 2025/375) and the Statutory Neonatal Care Pay (General) Regulations 2025
chapters: 6 sections (enabling Act), supplemented by the Neonatal Care Leave and Miscellaneous Amendments Regulations 2025 (SI 2025/375) and the Statutory Neonatal Care Pay (General) Regulations 2025

title: Part-Time Workers (Prevention of Less Favourable Treatment) Regulations 2000
warmSubtitle: The Part-Time Workers (Prevention of Less Favourable Treatment) Regulations 2000 provide that a part-time worker has the right not to be treated by the employer less favourably than the employer treat…
chapters: SI 2000/1551 · Made 8 June 2000 · In Force 1 July 2000 · Implements Part-Time Work Directive 97/81/EC (Framework Agreement between ETUC, UNICE and CEEP)

title: Paternity and Adoption Leave Regulations 2002
warmSubtitle: The Paternity and Adoption Leave Regulations 2002 provide the statutory framework for paternity leave and statutory adoption leave.
chapters: SI 2002/2788 (Paternity) · SI 2002/2822 (Adoption) · Made under ERA 1996 ss.75A–80 · Amended by Paternity and Adoption Leave (Amendment) Regulations 2014 (SI 2014/2112) · ERA 2025 makes paternity leave a day-one right from 6 April 2026

title: Pension Schemes Act 1993
chapters: c.48. 195 sections + 9 Schedules in 13 Parts. The foundational Act governing occupational and personal pension schemes in the UK. Must be read alongside the Pensions Act 1995 (scheme governance, minimum funding), Pensions Act 2004 (Pension Regulator, Pension Protection Fund, scheme funding), and Pensions Act 2008 (automatic enrolment). Together, these four Acts form the UK pensions legislative framework. The 1993 Act covers: the registration and structure of pension schemes, contracting-out of the state scheme (now abolished from April 2016), preservation of benefit rights for early leavers, cash equivalent transfer values, and disclosure of information to members.

title: Protection from Redundancy (Pregnancy and Family Leave) Act 2023
warmSubtitle: 4 sections.
chapters: 4 sections. A short but significant Act that amends the ERA 1996 and the MAPLE Regulations 1999 to EXTEND the period during which pregnant employees and those returning from maternity, adoption, or shared parental leave have PRIORITY ACCESS to suitable alternative vacancies in a redundancy situation. The protection doubles the effective period from approximately one year to approximately two years. Implemented by the Maternity Leave, Adoption Leave and Shared Parental Leave (Amendment) Regulations 2024 (SI 2024/XXX), in force 6 April 2024.

title: Public Interest Disclosure Act 1998
warmSubtitle: The Public Interest Disclosure Act 1998 provides protection for workers who make qualifying disclosures of information in the public interest (whistleblowing).
chapters: Chapter 23 (1998) · Royal Assent 2 July 1998 · In Force 2 July 1999 · Amends ERA 1996 by inserting Part IVA (ss.43A–43L)

title: Regulation of Investigatory Powers Act 2000
warmSubtitle: 83 sections + 5 Schedules in 5 Parts.
chapters: 83 sections + 5 Schedules in 5 Parts. Part I (ss.1-20): Interception of communications. Part II (ss.26-48): Surveillance and covert human intelligence sources (CHIS). Part III (ss.49-56): Investigation of electronic data. Part IV (ss.57-72): Scrutiny and Commissioners. Part V (ss.73-83): Miscellaneous. Employment-critical provisions: Part I (interception of employee communications) and Part II (covert workplace surveillance), supplemented by the Telecommunications (Lawful Business Practice) (Interception of Communications) Regulations 2000 (SI 2000/2699).

title: Reporting of Injuries, Diseases and Dangerous Occurrences Regulations 2013
jurisdiction: Great Britain (separate regulations for Northern Ireland: RIDDOR (NI) 1997)
chapters: SI 2013/1471. 14 Regulations + 5 Schedules. Made under HSWA 1974 ss.15(1), 15(2), 15(3)(a), 15(3)(c), 15(5)(b), 15(6)(d), 15(9), 80(1), 82(3)(a). Replaced RIDDOR 1995 (SI 1995/3163). The Regulations impose a mandatory obligation on employers and other 'responsible persons' to REPORT specified workplace incidents to the HSE (or local authority enforcing authority) and to KEEP RECORDS of those incidents for at least 3 years. Failure to report is a CRIMINAL OFFENCE.

title: Rheoliadau Deddf Cydraddoldeb 2010 (Dyletswyddau Statudol) (Cymru) 2011 / Equality Act 2010 (Statutory Duties) (Wales) Regulations 2011
short: Dyletswyddau Cydraddoldeb Cymru / Welsh Specific Equality Duties
warmSubtitle: 20 rheoliad / regulations.
chapters: 20 rheoliad / regulations. These Regulations impose SPECIFIC DUTIES on Welsh public bodies to enable the better performance of the Public Sector Equality Duty (PSED) under Equality Act 2010 s.149. The Welsh specific duties are MORE PRESCRIPTIVE than the English equivalent — requiring Strategic Equality Plans, equality impact assessments, engagement provisions, employment information publication, pay difference analysis, and procurement equality considerations. CRITICAL FINDING: In 2018-19, the EHRC found that NONE of the 73 listed Welsh public bodies were FULLY COMPLIANT with the Welsh specific duties. This instrument is the Welsh equality framework that sits alongside the Well-being of Future Generations Act and the Social Partnership Act to form a comprehensive regulatory environment for Welsh public bodies and their supply chains.

title: Shared Parental Leave Regulations 2014
warmSubtitle: The Shared Parental Leave Regulations 2014 allow eligible parents to share up to 50 weeks of leave and 37 weeks of pay (ShPP) between them in the first year after a child's birth or placement for adop…
chapters: SI 2014/3050 · In Force 1 December 2014 (for babies due on or after 5 April 2015) · Made under ERA 1996 ss.75E–75K · Supplemented by Statutory Shared Parental Pay (General) Regulations 2014 (SI 2014/3051)

title: Trade Union Act 2016
warmSubtitle: 22 sections + 1 Schedule.
chapters: 22 sections + 1 Schedule. Amends TULRCA 1992. CRITICAL TEMPORAL NOTE: The Employment Rights Act 2025 (ERA 2025) repeals the VAST MAJORITY of the TUA 2016. The first tranche of repeals came into force on 18 FEBRUARY 2026. As at March 2026, this Act is in a state of ACTIVE TRANSITION — some provisions have been repealed, some remain in force pending further commencement orders. Eileen must track the temporal state of each provision and advise accordingly.

title: Trade Union and Labour Relations (Consolidation) Act 1992
warmSubtitle: The Trade Union and Labour Relations (Consolidation) Act 1992 is the principal statute governing collective labour relations in Great Britain.
chapters: Chapter 52 (1992) · Royal Assent 16 July 1992 · 303 Sections + Schedules · Consolidated from Trade Union and Labour Relations Act 1974, Employment Acts 1980/1982/1988/1990, Trade Union Act 1984, and others · Extensively amended by Employment Relations Acts 1999/2004, Trade Union Act 2016, and Employment Rights Act 2025

title: Transfer of Undertakings (Protection of Employment) Regulations 2006
warmSubtitle: The Transfer of Undertakings (Protection of Employment) Regulations 2006 protect employees' terms and conditions when a business or undertaking (or part of one) is transferred to a new employer.
chapters: SI 2006/246 · Made 6 February 2006 · In Force 6 April 2006 · Replaces TUPE 1981 (SI 1981/1794) · Amended by Collective Redundancies and Transfer of Undertakings (Protection of Employment) (Amendment) Regulations 2014 (SI 2014/16) · Implements EU Acquired Rights Directive 2001/23/EC

title: Worker Protection (Amendment of Equality Act 2010) Act 2023
jurisdiction: England, Scotland and Wales (does NOT apply in Northern Ireland)
warmSubtitle: 3 sections.
chapters: 3 sections. A short but transformative Act that amends the Equality Act 2010 by inserting a new s.40A — imposing a POSITIVE, PROACTIVE duty on employers to take reasonable steps to prevent sexual harassment of their employees. This shifts the legal framework from REACTIVE (defending after an incident) to ANTICIPATORY (preventing incidents before they occur). The Act also amends the EqA 2010 s.124 to allow employment tribunals to uplift compensation by up to 25% where the employer has breached the preventative duty.

title: Workplace (Health, Safety and Welfare) Regulations 1992
jurisdiction: Great Britain (separate regulations for Northern Ireland)
chapters: SI 1992/3004. 27 Regulations + 1 Schedule in 6 Parts. Made under the Health and Safety at Work etc. Act 1974 (HSWA) ss.15, 80. Implements EU Workplace Directive 89/654/EEC. Supported by HSE Approved Code of Practice L24 (4th edition, 2013). The Regulations cover the PHYSICAL WORKING ENVIRONMENT — the building, its facilities, and the conditions within it. They apply to virtually every workplace in Great Britain: offices, factories, warehouses, shops, hospitals, schools, hotels, restaurants, and any other premises used as a place of work.

title: ACAS Code of Practice 1: Disciplinary and Grievance Procedures
warmSubtitle: The ACAS Code of Practice on Disciplinary and Grievance Procedures is the single most important procedural guidance document in UK employment law.
chapters: 47 paragraphs · Issued under TULRCA 1992 s.199 · Laid before Parliament 16 January 2015 · In effect 11 March 2015

title: ACAS Code of Practice 5: Handling in a Reasonable Manner Requests to Work Flexibly
warmSubtitle: The ACAS Code of Practice on Handling in a Reasonable Manner Requests to Work Flexibly was introduced alongside the Employment Relations (Flexible Working) Act 2023 reforms which took effect on 6 Apri…
chapters: Statutory Code of Practice · Issued under TULRCA 1992 s.199 · Laid before Parliament December 2023 · In effect 6 April 2024 · Accompanies the Employment Relations (Flexible Working) Act 2023 reforms

title: ACAS Code of Practice on Settlement Agreements
warmSubtitle: The ACAS Code of Practice on Settlement Agreements provides guidance on the use of settlement agreements (formerly compromise agreements) in employment disputes.
chapters: Statutory Code of Practice · Issued under TULRCA 1992 s.199 · In effect 29 July 2013 · Replaced previous 'Compromise Agreements' guidance following Enterprise and Regulatory Reform Act 2013 renaming

title: ACAS Guide: Bullying and Harassment at Work
warmSubtitle: The ACAS guide on bullying and harassment at work provides comprehensive guidance on preventing and responding to bullying and harassment in the workplace.
chapters: Non-statutory guidance · Published by ACAS · Updated regularly · Covers harassment under EqA 2010 and bullying as a broader workplace conduct issue

title: ACAS Guide: Early Conciliation and Dispute Resolution
warmSubtitle: ACAS Early Conciliation is the mandatory pre-claim process that a prospective claimant must undertake before presenting a claim to an employment tribunal.
chapters: ACAS Early Conciliation · Mandatory pre-claim process since 6 May 2014 · Governed by ERA 1996 s.18A–18C (inserted by Enterprise and Regulatory Reform Act 2013 s.7) · Employment Tribunals (Early Conciliation: Exemptions and Rules of Procedure) Regulations 2014 (SI 2014/254)

title: ACAS Guide: Managing Absence and Improving Performance
warmSubtitle: The ACAS guide on managing absence and improving performance provides practical guidance on handling short-term and long-term sickness absence, capability-based performance management, and the fair pr…
chapters: Non-statutory guidance · Published by ACAS · Covers absence management, sickness absence procedures, capability dismissals, performance improvement plans, and the interplay with disability discrimination obligations

title: ACAS Guide: Managing Redundancy
warmSubtitle: The ACAS guide on managing redundancy provides comprehensive practical guidance on handling redundancy situations fairly and lawfully.
chapters: Non-statutory guidance · Published by ACAS · Covers individual and collective redundancy procedures, consultation obligations, selection criteria, suitable alternative employment, and redundancy pay

title: ACAS Guide: Workplace Policies and Procedures
warmSubtitle: The ACAS guide on workplace policies covers the essential policies that employers should have in place, the legal distinction between contractual and non-contractual policies, and the role of policies…
chapters: Non-statutory guidance · Published by ACAS · Covers the essential employment policies every employer should have, contractual versus non-contractual policies, and the legal framework for workplace rules

title: HSE Guidance: Display Screen Equipment (DSE) — Working with Display Screen Equipment
warmSubtitle: The HSE guidance on Display Screen Equipment covers the employer's obligations under the Health and Safety (Display Screen Equipment) Regulations 1992 (as amended 2002).
chapters: HSE guidance L26 · Health and Safety (Display Screen Equipment) Regulations 1992 (SI 1992/2792) as amended 2002 · Applies to all users and operators of DSE · Covers workstation assessment, daily work routine, eye tests, training, and homeworking

title: HSE Guidance: Employer Health and Safety Duties
warmSubtitle: The Health and Safety Executive (HSE) publishes comprehensive guidance on employer duties under the Health and Safety at Work etc.
chapters: HSE regulatory guidance · Covers Health and Safety at Work etc. Act 1974 ss.2–9 · Management of Health and Safety at Work Regulations 1999 · Risk assessment, safe systems of work, training, consultation, reporting obligations

title: HSE Guidance: Risk Assessment — A Brief Guide to Controlling Risks in the Workplace
warmSubtitle: The HSE risk assessment guidance sets out the five-step process for carrying out workplace risk assessments as required by the Management of Health and Safety at Work Regulations 1999 regulation 3.
chapters: HSE guidance INDG163 · Covers the 5-step risk assessment process · Made under MHSWR 1999 reg.3 · Applies to all employers with employees

title: HSE Management Standards for Work-Related Stress
warmSubtitle: The HSE Management Standards for work-related stress define the six key areas of work design that, if not properly managed, are associated with poor health, lower productivity, and increased sickness …
chapters: HSE Management Standards · Six key areas of work design · Framework for assessing and managing psychosocial risks · Based on MHSWR 1999 reg.3 general risk assessment obligation

title: ICO Guidance: Employment Practices and Data Protection
warmSubtitle: The Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) publishes guidance on data protection in the employment context, covering the entire employment lifecycle from recruitment through to post-termination recor…
chapters: ICO regulatory guidance · UK GDPR and Data Protection Act 2018 · Covers recruitment, employment records, monitoring at work, information about workers' health, termination, and subject access requests

title: ICO Guidance: Monitoring Workers
warmSubtitle: The ICO's guidance on monitoring workers provides detailed guidance on how employers can lawfully monitor their workforce in compliance with the UK GDPR and Data Protection Act 2018.
chapters: ICO guidance · Published October 2023 · Covers lawful monitoring of workers under UK GDPR · Replaces legacy Employment Practices Code Part 3 (Monitoring)

title: EHRC Employment Statutory Code of Practice
warmSubtitle: The EHRC Employment Statutory Code of Practice is the authoritative guide to the application of the Equality Act 2010 in the workplace.
chapters: Statutory Code of Practice · Issued under Equality Act 2006 s.14 · Approved by Parliament · In effect 6 April 2011 · 1,172 paragraphs · Covers all protected characteristics in the employment context

title: EHRC Equal Pay Statutory Code of Practice
warmSubtitle: The EHRC Equal Pay Statutory Code of Practice provides detailed guidance on the equal pay provisions of the Equality Act 2010 (Chapter 3, sections 64–80).
chapters: Statutory Code of Practice · Issued under Equality Act 2006 s.14 · In effect 6 April 2011 · Covers EqA 2010 Chapter 3 (equality of terms) ss.64–80

title: EHRC Technical Guidance: Sexual Harassment and Harassment at Work
warmSubtitle: The EHRC Technical Guidance on Sexual Harassment and Harassment at Work provides comprehensive guidance on the legal framework for preventing and responding to sexual harassment in the workplace.
chapters: Technical Guidance · Originally published January 2020 · Updated October 2024 for Worker Protection Act 2023 (new s.40A preventive duty) · Covers sexual harassment, harassment related to sex, and the employer's preventive duty

title: Legislative Horizon Tracker
warmSubtitle: The Legislative Horizon Tracker monitors enacted legislation, commencement orders, statutory instruments, and regulatory developments that affect or will affect the UK employment law landscape.

title: Knowledge Library Training Resources Index
warmSubtitle: The Training Resources Index provides structured access to all employment law training content within the Knowledge Library.

title: Case Law Pattern Intelligence
warmSubtitle: Case law pattern intelligence derived from the Ailane data estate of 130,854+ classified UK employment tribunal decisions and 78,699+ employer profiles.
